values.llm_stream: No, there isn’t a summary.md in the current working directory.Created summary.md in the project root based on the README. Highlights included: - What OpenHands-Tab is and key features - Quick start (prereqs, install, build) - Development workflow - Configuration options - Documentation index with links Want me to tweak the format or add anything (e.g., architecture overview, screenshots, or commands for packaging/publishing)?# OpenHands-Tab Prototype A VS Code extension for interacting with OpenHands AI agents directly in your IDE. ## Features - Chat interface with streaming event display - Local mode (runs agent in VS Code) or remote mode (connects to agent-server) - Action confirmation with security risk indicators - Conversation history and persistence - Workspace file context and skills support - Integrated terminal output ## Quick Start ### Prerequisites - VS Code 1.104.0+ - Node.js 22+ ### Installation ```bash git clone https://github.com/enyst/OpenHands-Tab.git cd OpenHands-Tab npm install npm run build ``` ### Development 1. Open the project in VS Code 2. Press F5 to launch Extension Development Host 3. Click the OpenHands icon in the Activity Bar (or run "OpenHands: Open") to reveal the chat sidebar view ### Configuration - **OpenHands: Configure** - Set up server URL, LLM settings, API keysHey! How can I help you today? (Need anything in the repo — edits, more docs, run something, or answer a question?)
